page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc6.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated physical cores running at a base clock speed of 3.1 GHz with one thread per core. The instance is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage and KVM virtualization. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations place this instance in the average performance tier across several workloads. The Intel Xeon processor achieves a single-core stress-ng CPU score of 1435.39 and a multi-core score of 30631.79. In memory bandwidth tests, the system registers 1704.01 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 104.96 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Cryptographic and vector processing capabilities are supported by AVX-512 and AES instruction sets, reflected in a Passmark encryption score of 14152.65. For specialized tasks, the instance processes 12.14 tokens per second for text generation on a 7B LLM and handles 29.05 million Redis SET operations per second.

From a resource density perspective, the ecs.hfc6.8xlarge offers a high compute-to-memory ratio, making it a cost-efficient option for CPU-bound tasks that do not require massive memory footprints. However, the lack of local storage and GPU hardware represents a clear tradeoff, limiting its utility for large-scale database hosting or heavy deep learning training. The instance is best suited for workloads such as software compilation, multi-threaded decompression, static web serving, and moderate-scale vector calculations.

Built on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 64.0 GB of system memory, establishing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, utilizing KVM virtualization and a 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks show a multi-core stress-ng score of 30631.79 and a Passmark CPU score of 29137.73. It delivers 29.05 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and processes 7B LLM text generation at 12.14 tokens per second. This hardware profile makes the instance cost-efficient for CPU-heavy, memory-light workloads. It is suited for software compilation, multi-threaded decompression, and static web serving.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc6.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 32 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 64 GB RAM, and a 10 Gbps network baseline, designed for CPU-intensive workloads without local storage.
